label: Notion
description: Read and write Notion pages, databases, and blocks from your workspace.

form.title: Connect Notion
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to read and write pages and databases in your Notion workspace.

methods.label: Internal Integration Secret
methods.description: Paste an Internal Integration token from your Notion workspace. The simplest path — no OAuth flow required, just create an integration and copy the secret.
methods.how_to:
## Create a Notion Internal Integration

Notion internal integrations are workspace-scoped API keys that you create in seconds. No OAuth client setup needed.

### Steps

1. Open [Notion](https://www.notion.so) and navigate to **Settings & Members** (bottom-left sidebar or top-right avatar menu).
2. Select **Connections** in the left sidebar, then click **Develop or manage integrations**. This opens `https://www.notion.so/my-integrations`.
3. Click **+ New integration**.
4. Give it a name (e.g. `Anton`), choose your workspace, and optionally upload a logo.
5. Under **Capabilities**, select the permissions Anton needs:
   - **Read content** — to read pages and databases
   - **Update content** — to write back to pages
   - **Insert content** — to create new pages or blocks
6. Click **Save**. Notion displays the **Internal Integration Secret** (starts with `secret_`).
7. Click **Show** and then copy the token. Paste it into the **Integration Secret** field below.

### Connect pages and databases

Notion integrations only see content you explicitly share with them:
1. Open any page or database you want Anton to access.
2. Click **⋯ More** (top-right) → **Add connections** → find your integration and click **Confirm**.

### Notes

- The secret is shown immediately after creation. If you lose it, regenerate it from the same integrations page.
- Rotating the secret invalidates the old one — paste the new secret into Anton to reconnect.
- Read [Notion's authorization guide](https://developers.notion.com/docs/authorization) for full details.

fields.name: integration_secret
fields.label: Integration Secret
fields.description: Found at notion.so/my-integrations → your integration → Internal Integration Secret. Starts with `secret_`.

fields.name: notion_version
fields.label: Notion API Version
fields.description: The Notion-Version header to use. Defaults to the current stable version (2022-06-28).

methods.label: OAuth (Public Integration)
methods.description: Use a public Notion OAuth integration with your own Client ID and Secret. Best for multi-workspace deployments or when distributing Anton to other Notion users.
methods.how_to:
## Set up a Notion Public OAuth Integration

Public integrations allow users to authorize Anton via a browser consent screen. Requires creating a public integration in the Notion developer portal.

### Steps

1. Go to [notion.so/my-integrations](https://www.notion.so/my-integrations) and click **+ New integration**.
2. Under **Integration type**, select **Public**.
3. Fill in the required fields: name, logo, company, website, and **Redirect URIs** (use `http://127.0.0.1` for local desktop use).
4. Set the **Capabilities** your integration needs (Read content, Update content, Insert content).
5. Click **Save**. Copy the **OAuth client ID** and **OAuth client secret** from the **Secrets** section.
6. Paste both into the fields below and click **Submit**. A browser window will open for workspace authorization.
7. After granting access, Notion redirects back and Anton stores the token.

### Notes

- Public integrations require Notion review before they can be used by workspaces you don't own. For personal use, stick with the **Internal Integration Secret** method.
- Notion OAuth does not use standard `scope` query parameters — capabilities are configured on the integration itself.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From notion.so/my-integrations → your public integration → OAuth client ID.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: From notion.so/my-integrations → your public integration → OAuth client secret.
